concerns.sourceDodItem: Scope 2 scopes.md DoD 321a (Scope 2-owned ingest sub-budget stress assertion via `TestQFDecisionsFreshnessSLAP95IngestRender`)
concerns.followUpAction: Extend `tests/stress/qf_decision_event_replay_test.go::TestQFDecisionsFreshnessSLAP95IngestRender` so the drive loop actually sustains live ingest activity for >= 60s (the duration the file header comment advertises as a CEILING). Two viable approaches: (a) re-drive the cursor back to the first page once `cursorOrder[len(cursorOrder)-1]` is reached, so the loop can keep generating fixture traffic until `time.Now().Before(deadline)` is false; (b) extend `jitterMaxMillis` from 500 to something like 2500-5000 so per-page latency forces the loop to span >= 60s naturally. Either approach must preserve the PASS verdict (ingest p95 still <= 30s) and the bonus adversarial trip-wire (packetFetches == totalArtifactsDriven). After the change, re-run via the same scope-isolated stress invocation and update the Round 8 evidence section with the new duration.
concerns.rationale: Added by Round 8 bubbles.iterate after bubbles.test's HONEST DISCLOSURE. The current test exits the drive loop via the second clause `cursor != cursorOrder[len(cursorOrder)-1]` as soon as cursor exhaustion is reached -- approximately 10s in -- not when the 75s deadline elapses. The file header's '>= 60s sustained' is therefore a CEILING (an upper bound the loop won't exceed) not a FLOOR (a guaranteed minimum). This does NOT invalidate the Round 8 PASS verdict (the ingest sub-budget assertion still holds for the 10s of live traffic) but it does mean the test is less stressful than the file header suggests. Tracking as a separate low-severity concern so Scope 2's DoD 321a remains legitimately `[x]` while the test-fidelity gap can be addressed by future hardening without re-opening the closed sub-DoD.
